---
title: "About"
---

::: {.hero}
<span class="eyebrow">Founded 1991</span>

## Every object embeds a decision
:::

## Our mission

The Werkbank Museum collects, preserves, and exhibits the objects of design
and technology that shaped how people work, communicate, and live. We
believe everyday objects deserve the same close looking as fine art: every
switch, seat, and circuit board embeds a decision about how it should feel
to be human.

## History

The museum grew out of a private collection of typewriters and drafting
tools assembled by engineer and design historian Elsa Reinhardt starting in
the 1970s. What began as a personal archive in a converted workshop
("Werkbank" is German for workbench) opened to the public in 1991 and has
since expanded to cover computing, furniture, and household technology.

## Visiting

For hours, admission, and directions, see the [Visit](visit.qmd) page.
